steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank is a transition word opening the sentence after one that establishes a specific location (the wall of a university building in the United States). The following sentence describes how the sculpture looks at that location. The blank must link the next sentence back to that place.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'There,': means "in/at that place" — points directly back to the university wall just named. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Later,': signals time progression, but no time shift is happening — the next sentence describes the same moment at the same place. ✗
  • Choice C — 'For instance,': introduces an example of a broader category, but the passage is discussing this specific sculpture, not illustrating a general point. ✗
  • Choice D — 'However,': signals contrast, but the next sentence simply continues describing the sculpture at its established location — no contradiction. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
